title: How to tell a patient they're overdone
filename: How to tell a patient they_re overdone.en.vtt
excerpt: So here's a dilemma we're all faced with this as injectors and even as just friends of people who have aesthetic treatments what if someone is clearly overdone and is going around thinking they look amazing but they actually don't how would you handle that situation this is a rea…

segments.text: so here's a dilemma we're all faced with this as injectors and even as just friends of people who have aesthetic treatments what if someone is clearly overdone and is going around thinking they look amazing but they actually don't how would you handle that situation this is a really interesting topic because we all face it at various points and it's tricky because none of us want to cause harm to patients even though they already are overdoing themselves how do you handle that so why is this tricky i think it's tricky because we are all intuitively aware that there are multiple factors that make someone happy and it is possible that someone could be over treated and happy and then you could tell them about their over treatment and it could make them less happy in which case you've done some harm to them and we don't want to take that risk of course if you're the bearer of bad news even if you're correct and they end up having a better treatment it could still harm your relationship because no one actually likes to be criticized so this

is another factor that stops people speaking up finally there may be some small dark part of you who actually wants to criticize them maybe they've offended you in the past and you just want to use some way of getting back at them disguising it as some sort of concern i think that happens too sadly so how do we approach this first is be really clear on what your ultimate goal is for me the only reason why you would do this is because you have the other person's interests at heart make sure that you're at that place before you even attempt any of this because it's really important to get it right that they are aware that you're acting in their best interests and that you indeed actually are doing that so what are the decision making processes behind the scenes here i believe that we are all trying to orient ourselves using different methods so if you took the aesthetic model on its own then your only justification for telling someone that they are over treated is just that they are over treated and you make the justification to yourself that you are officially and technically correct and therefore you can tell them whatever you like and be done with how it affects them

psychologically because you have the aesthetic rules on your side now i know there are many people in this industry and people out there in the world who will use this as the sole justification and feel very confident in their ability to do so and but i think maybe we should consider the other ways of making the decision too so let's consider the social justification so this would be the social model of decision making where your justification is that other people are not reacting well to this person and this is certainly a valid concern because as i've said in many videos before humans are all about collaboration our ability to collaborate with people is vital to our survival so if your concern is that the patient is no longer going to be able to collaborate well with other people because they look so over treated this could be a valid reason to interject however you must also consider the psychological justification for this now if the patient is really happy and loving life but some people don't like their face this could still be on

balance better for them to have over treatment than if you were to completely have no treatment for example knock out their confidence and then everyone else is fine with them but they also not aren't collaborating well because they don't look like a confident person this is where it gets tricky and this is the final model to consider which is the psychological model and most people use this as a justification for over treatment which is if the patient is happy i will over treat them because as long as they're happy then i've justified my particular intervention this is also too narrow if you use it on its own and this is why what we really need to do is to combine all of these together and that is what essentially the medical model is so we are making a decision with the patient's overall health at the center of it and this is how you should approach a patient who's having over treatment now the problem is if they're not a patient for example if it's your mum or your auntie they are not actually seeking your advice and this is where it gets most difficult how do you tell someone or try to guide someone or influence someone when in fact they are not asking

for your help and in their world everything is fine for me this starts a lot with how i would deal with it in the consultation you have to start exploring their relationship with their face in a way that is on their side so we're not doing this in a judgmental way but you're actually curious ask that auntie or your mom who's had a bit too much what does it mean to them to actually have their treatment what is it that they love about that result that conversation will immediately hopefully get them to open up and be positive about it depends a lot on how you ask it but if you phrase it well and you're genuinely curious you'll get them talking about the topic the next thing is if you actually want to influence them a really good tip in life in general is to ask for permission to do that rather than just do it so if you walk up to someone in the street and say you know your haircut isn't quite right at the back they're likely to be offended no matter how right you are about the haircuts but if you create a situation where you'd like where you ask them for permission to give them some feedback

they are just a little bit more open to it and the same with faces so can i give you some feedback would you be open to some feedback about something that i've noticed or would you rather manage this all on your own now that's still an awkward question granted but it's better than just saying i think it looks overdone because it's got them already open and looking for the answer themselves so ask for permission and then when you start to give them permission a great tip i learned when i started managing people is to give them what is often called a sandwich now what that is something good something bad and something good so you start with something good like i think you really maintain yourself really well and i really love certain treatments than the bit but i think you're a little bit over treated in your lips um and then the good bit if you were to get that right i think you're doing amazing for your age it's that kind of structure of something good something bad and something good that overall will preserve your relationship with the person and make them feel that you genuinely care about them but also give

them the message that they need to hear of course this is only once they've given you permission to actually give you that statement now overall just another reminder that we are not only obsessed with the aesthetic it is possible that someone could be happier and better off and over treated in some certain circumstances and it's also possible that they are completely oblivious and that things are much worse for them than they realize and you could do them a great service by having this conversation but we need to pay attention and think about all those domains of health the psychology the aesthetic result and how they're engaging with people in life and make sure that you're genuinely on their side and not just criticizing them i'm sure most of you will like that if you've got their best interests at heart the average person will be grateful if you're on the money and your advice is good if you've done it in the right way but don't walk up to people in the street and tell them i think that's a little bit harsh so this is obviously it's much harder if they are not your patient if they are your patient implicit in that

arrangement is that you are to give them some advice i think you still need to structure it carefully so as not to cause harm but at the same time that's a lot easier than just someone who you know or someone who you see in the street who you think needs your help now here i would say my biggest advice would be is to be humble in that situation humans are complex what makes them happy is complex there are plenty of happy people who have body modifications you know tattoos all sorts of things which make them happy but are not part of the medical model and it's also possible to do aesthetic treatments in a way that isn't part of the medical model so people who look obviously augmented could in theory be better off for that so stay humble investigate it carefully try and separate out the psychology the social and the aesthetic and try your best to always act in people's best interests and be delicate with it but i'm interested have you got friends and family who are over treated who you're desperate to say something about have you tried it and got some sort of feedback either good or bad let
me know in the comments down below [Music] you
